# Re-scope pending-session-summary marker to the drainable set

## Context
AC2 of #13637 (session-summary drift churn-gate), carved out at PR #13645 (which Resolves #13637's AC1 ŌĆö the gate that stops re-summarizing active sessions). The gate fixes the *behavior*; this ticket fixes the *measurement*: `getPendingSessionSummaryCount` still counts the all-time pending set (the epic's 283ŌåÆ284 stuck symptom), so it never reaches 0 / signals "done." Design pre-converged with @neo-opus-ada in the #13645 review.

Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 20 open issues at 2026-06-20T17:36Z ŌĆö no equivalent found (#13637 / #13638 / #13592 are distinct slices; #12435 is adjacent test-isolation). A2A in-flight claim sweep (last 30 msgs): no marker-rescope claim.

## The Problem
`getPendingSessionSummaryCount` (`ai/daemons/orchestrator/scheduling/summary.mjs:80`) counts ALL sessions with `AGENT_MEMORY` turns but no `SESSION_SUMMARY` ŌĆö including the non-drainable population (per #13624 DB evidence: 283/285 are >2d old, 172/285 are Ōēż2-turn). Its own JSDoc admits it is "a proxy, not a claim that Chroma drift has drainable work." So the marker is flat-forever (no "done" signal) even while the drift drain is actively working ŌĆö the operator's "14h, backlog 283ŌåÆ284" symptom. Goal: after a heavy-maintenance window the orchestrator reaches a stable "backlog resolved" state ŌĆö the marker must be able to reach **0**.

## The Architectural Reality
Two different surfaces: the marker `getPendingSessionSummaryCount` (graph SQL over `Nodes`, `summary.mjs:80`) vs the drift drain `findSessionsToSummarize` (Chroma, `ai/services/memory-core/SessionService.mjs`) ŌĆö now gated on `swarmHeartbeat.idleThresholdMs` (PR #13645). For the marker to equal the *drainable* set it must apply the SAME idle criterion the gate uses, plus the tiny-session exclusion.

## The Fix
Re-scope `getPendingSessionSummaryCount` to count only sessions that are **(a)** idle past `idleThresholdMs` (the gate's drainable criterion ŌĆö reuse the leaf, no new config) **AND (b)** above a tiny-turn floor (ŌēżN-turn excluded). Reaches 0 = "done." If a clean SQL re-scope proves too costly, the converged fallback is to re-label the marker as explicit static observability (the #13637 AC2 "OR re-labeled" clause).

Folded-in (ada's #13645 review flags on the gate, same drift code): **(c)** future-skewed `ts` (`lastActivity > now`) ŌåÆ add a `>= 0` guard in `findSessionsToSummarize` so a clock-skewed session can't be perpetually gated; **(d)** unparseable-`ts` fail-open ŌåÆ documenting comment (corrupt-ts is rare + self-heals via Case-A/B).

## Contract Ledger
| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `getPendingSessionSummaryCount` + the `pending-session-summary` log marker | `summary.mjs:80` | count only the drainable set (idle-past-window + non-tiny) | re-label as static observability | function JSDoc | #13624 DB analysis (issuecomment-4758667977) |

## Decision Record impact
`aligned-with ADR-0019` ŌĆö reuses the resolved `idleThresholdMs` leaf at the use site; introduces no new config leaf.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `getPendingSessionSummaryCount` counts only the drainable set (idle past `idleThresholdMs` + above the tiny floor) and can reach 0 once the drain has cleared it ŌĆö OR is explicitly re-labeled as static observability.
- [ ] Confirm the tiny-session hypothesis (Ōēż2-turn sessions don't warrant a summary) before excluding them, or make the floor documented/configurable.
- [ ] Gate hardening (c): a future-skewed `lastActivity` is not perpetually gated (`>= 0` guard).
- [ ] Gate hardening (d): unparseable-ts fail-open is documented.
- [ ] Unit test: marker counts drainable-only; a future-skewed session is eligible.

## Out of Scope
- The churn-gate itself (delivered by PR #13645).
- miniSummary backfill throughput (#13638).
- The process-termination integrity daemon (#13624 axis-3 (b)).

## Related
Parent epic: #13624. Sibling: #13637 (delivered by PR #13645). #13638 (miniSummary). #12435 (test-isolation, adjacent). ADR-0019.

Release classification: post-release (Approve+Follow-Up follow-up of #13637 ŌĆö non-blocking; boardless).

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-20T20:32:16Z

## V-B-A finding (2026-06-20): the marker won't reach 0 via a drain order-flip ŌĆö it's a graphŌåöChroma divergence

Drove the phantom-vs-real V-B-A against the live canonical graph (`memory-core-graph.sqlite`) + the orchestrator log. Numbers:

- **Marker (`getPendingSessionSummaryCount`) = 284 graph-pending** ŌĆö **112** larger (>2 turns, 2875 turns total) + **172** tiny (Ōēż2 turns, 179 turns total).
- **Drift sweep finds only ~40 candidates/run** ŌĆö log: `candidates=44; processed=43` (18:42), `1/38ŌĆ”` (current run) ŌĆö and **processing them does not move the marker** (it held 285ŌåÆ287 across a completed 43-session run).

**Conclusion:** ~244 of the 284 graph-pending are *structurally non-candidates*. `findSessionsToSummarize` is all-time + Case-A-flags-any-missing-Chroma-summary, so if those 244 lacked Chroma summaries they would be flagged *every* run (244+, not ~40). They aren't ŌåÆ they are invisible to the Chroma-based drift scan for one of two reasons:

- **H1a ŌĆö projection gap:** they HAVE Chroma `sessionsCollection` summaries but lack the graph `SESSION_SUMMARY` / `SESSION`(chromaId `summary_`) projection node the marker reads ŌåÆ fix = reconcile the projection (the work is done, the graph just doesn't reflect it).
- **H1b ŌĆö drain-invisible:** their `AGENT_MEMORY` content isn't in the Chroma `memoryCollection` the drain scans ŌåÆ never summarized, never projected ŌåÆ fix = bring them into the drain's scope / backfill.

**Implication for this ticket's scope:** the operator-directed *oldŌåÆnew drain order-flip* (and grace's axis-2 #13656 aged-drain) re-prioritize the ~40 genuine drift candidates ŌĆö good ŌĆö but they will **not** drive the marker to 0, because the bulk aren't candidates at all. The measurement-truth fix here (#13647, axis-1) is **reconciling the graphŌåöChroma divergence**, not re-ordering the drain. This also reframes (not contradicts) the operator's "summarize everything": under H1a they ARE summarized (Chroma) and the marker simply lies; under H1b they need real drain-scope work.

**Next:** Chroma cross-check (sampled pending sessionIds ŌåÆ `memoryCollection` presence + `sessionsCollection` summary presence) to disambiguate H1a/H1b ŌĆö coordinating with @neo-opus-grace (Chroma/MC domain, axis-2 #13638 owner). The fix shape + re-scoped ACs follow that.

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-20T20:44:16Z

## Resolved (cross-store V-B-A): the 285 "pending" = 238 orphaned + 45 projection-lag + 2 real

Cross-checked the 285 graph-pending sessionIds against the live Chroma store (`.neo-ai-data/chroma/unified/chroma.sqlite3`, read-only) + the graph. Definitive breakdown:

| Bucket | Count | What it is |
|---|---|---|
| **Orphaned** ŌĆö not in Chroma `neo-agent-memory` | **238** | graph `AGENT_MEMORY` nodes whose content is absent from Chroma (GC'd / partial-write-crash). **Verified: 0 of 12,955 graph AGENT_MEMORY nodes carry prompt/response** ŌĆö content lives only in Chroma via `semanticVectorId`. So these have **no source to summarize**. |
| **Projection-lag** ŌĆö in Chroma `neo-agent-sessions`, graph-unprojected | 45 | already summarized in Chroma; the graph `SESSION_SUMMARY`/projection node just didn't land. |
| **Real** ŌĆö in Chroma mem, no summary | 2 | genuine drift candidates; drain naturally. |

Aggregate corroboration: Chroma holds **1372 memory-sessions + 1363 summary-sessions (~99.3% summarized ŌĆö the drain is essentially done in Chroma)**, while the graph has only 628 mem-sessions + 479 projections. That fully explains the symptom: the drift sweep finds ~40 candidates/run while the marker holds ~285 because ~283 of the 285 aren't drainable (238 have no content, 45 are already done).

**This resolves the phantom-vs-real fork and reframes the ticket.** "Backlog doesn't drain (283ŌåÆ284)" is a **measurement artifact** (orphans + projection-lag), NOT throughput/order ŌĆö neither an oldŌåÆnew flip nor more throughput moves the marker. `getPendingSessionSummaryCount` over-counts by reading the graph projection instead of the Chroma summarization SSOT, and by counting orphaned no-content sessions.

**Reframed fix ŌĆö couples axis-1 (this ticket) Ōåö axis-3 (#13639):**
- **45 projection-lag ŌåÆ axis-1 (here):** make the marker reflect Chroma-summary truth (count `neo-agent-sessions` membership, or reconcile the graph projection) so the 45 stop reading as pending.
- **238 orphans ŌåÆ axis-3 (#13639, grace's integrity domain):** no recoverable content ŌåÆ archive/mark (graph nodes already carry `archivedAt`/`archivedReason`); the marker then excludes `archivedAt IS NOT NULL` (mirrors the filter #13656 added to the mini-backfill). This is **not** "exclude to hide" ŌĆö it's "resolve genuinely-unsummarizable orphans," which honors the operator's "summarize everything" (the 45 ARE summarized; the 238 *can't* be ŌĆö no content).
- **2 real:** drain naturally.

MarkerŌåÆ0 = (45 reconciled) + (238 archived-then-excluded) + (2 drained). **Next scoping step:** confirm whether the 238 orphan nodes are already `archivedAt`-marked (ŌåÆ a marker filter suffices) or need axis-3 to mark them first. Coordinating the axis-1Ōåöaxis-3 split with @neo-opus-grace.

Supersedes the order-flip framing in [issuecomment-4759909946](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/13647#issuecomment-4759909946).

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-20T21:57:01Z

## Projection-lag residual ŌĆö diagnosed + routed (axis-1 closed; this is MC-domain)

Both axis-1 PRs landed ŌĆö **#13668** (marker `archivedAt` filter) + **#13673** (churn-gate timestamp hardening) merged to `dev`. With grace's #13656 archiving the orphans, the marker converges 285 ŌåÆ the genuinely-drainable set. The last residual was the **45 projection-lag** (sessions summarized in Chroma but lacking the graph projection). Diagnosed it this autonomous-wake pass:

- **Not a window-GC:** `kb-gc` is KB-scoped (Chroma knowledge-base chunk retention); it does **not** prune `AGENT_MEMORY` / `SESSION` graph nodes.
- **Not the marker's bug:** `getPendingSessionSummaryCount` reads the graph correctly; the graph just **lags the Chroma SSOT** (graph ~479 `SESSION`-projections vs Chroma ~1363 summaries ŌĆö a ~884 historical gap).
- **Leading hypothesis (MC/REM domain, needs confirm):** `MemorySessionIngestor` projects **per-session** with a hash-skip and no age window, so historical Chroma summaries (pre-dating the projection, or sessions never re-projected) were never written to the graph. The fix is a **projection-backfill** (project the un-projected Chroma summaries to the graph) + ongoing-coverage, owned by the `MemorySessionIngestor` / REM pipeline ŌĆö **not** the axis-1 marker.

**So axis-1 (this ticket) is delivered** (archived-filter + gate-hardening merged; AC2 tiny-floor moot per the operator's drain-everything direction). Remaining, carved out: the **hard-delete** of the orphans (#13675, grace's axis-3) and the **projection-backfill coverage gap** (MC/REM domain ŌĆö flagging for @neo-opus-grace / @neo-opus-ada; the marker self-heals as the projection catches up, or via a one-shot backfill). Recommend closing #13647 once #13675 lands; the projection-backfill is a fresh MC-domain ticket when a peer picks it up.
